"get_team_logo_bulletproof"
"is_top_25_cbb"
"get_cbb_team_rank"
"is_bovada_game"
"api_line_movement_updates"
"download_app"
"get_matchup_data"
\)

    echo "=== $name ==="

Bash(/tmp/comprehensive_dead_code.py << 'PYTHON'
import re
import os

# Read sports_app.py
with open\('/home/runner/workspace/sports_app.py', 'r'\) as f:
    content = f.read\(\)
    lines = content.split\('\\n'\)

# Find all function and class definitions with line numbers
definitions = {}
for i, line in enumerate\(lines, 1\):
    # Match def or class at the start of a line \(possibly with whitespace\)
    match = re.match\(r'^\\s*\(def|class\)\\s+\(\\w+\)', line\)
    if match:
        kind = match.group\(1\)
        name = match.group\(2\)
        definitions[name] = \(i, kind\)

# Now check each one for actual references \(not just the definition\)
import subprocess

dead_code = []

for name, \(line_num, kind\) in sorted\(definitions.items\(\), key=lambda x: x[1][0]\):
    # Search in sports_app.py for references
    cmd = f"grep -n '\\\\b{name}\\\\b' /home/runner/workspace/sports_app.py"
    result = subprocess.run\(cmd, shell=True, capture_output=True, text=True\)
    lines_with_ref = result.stdout.strip\(\).split\('\\n'\) if result.stdout else []
    
    # Filter to only actual references \(not the definition line itself\)
    # Skip lines that are just the definition
    actual_refs = []
    for ref_line in lines_with_ref:
        if not ref_line:
            continue
        parts = ref_line.split\(':', 1\)
        if len\(parts\) == 2:
            try:
                ref_line_num = int\(parts[0]\)
                # Skip if it's the definition line or a comment line with the name
                if ref_line_num != line_num:
                    # Also skip if it's a docstring or comment with just the word
                    ref_text = parts[1].strip\(\)
                    # Skip pure comments/docstrings that just mention the name
                    if not ref_text.startswith\('#'\) and not ref_text.startswith\('"""'\) and not ref_text.startswith\("'''"\):
                        actual_refs.append\(ref_line_num\)
            except:
                pass
    
    if len\(actual_refs\) == 0:
        # Check if it's a Flask route handler
        is_route = False
        # Look back from definition line to find decorators
        for check_line in range\(max\(0, line_num-5\), line_num\):
            if '@app.route' in lines[check_line] or '@app.get' in lines[check_line] or '@app.post' in lines[check_line]:
                is_route = True
                break
        
        # Also skip magic methods and class definitions that are probably base/important
        if not is_route and name not in ['Base', 'Game', 'Pick', 'db', '__init__', '__str__', '__repr__']:
            dead_code.append\(\(name, line_num, kind, is_route\)\)

# Print results
for name, line_num, kind, is_route in dead_code:
    print\(f"{name:40} | Line {line_num:5} | {kind:5} | Route: {is_route}"\)

PYTHON)
